title: Hieracium stenogrammum
intro: Hieracium stenogrammum is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, known for its unique and delicate appearance. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various health issues. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium stenogrammum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and other health conditions.
taxonomy: Hieracium stenogrammum belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Hieracium. The genus Hieracium is part of the tribe Cichorieae within the family Asteraceae. The species is native to Europe, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships within the Asteraceae family. As a member of the tribe Cichorieae, Hieracium stenogrammum shares characteristics with other hawkweed species.
